findings.evidence: Existing general API: `ConditionallyIIDWith.map` at `ConditionallyIID.lean:92-95` states `blockLaw μ X k = ...` for every injective `k`; this PR repeats it at `ConditionallyIIDImplications.lean:48-53` only for permuted prefixes and then proves the natural general `blockLaw_eq_prefixLaw_of_injective` at lines 66-70.
findings.fix: Remove this public theorem or make it private/local; prove `exchangeableAt` via `h.blockLaw_eq_prefixLaw_of_injective (injective_perm_val σ)` after the general `blockLaw_eq_prefixLaw_of_injective`.
findings.issue: `ConditionallyIIDWith.permuted_prefixLaw_eq_mixture` is only the `k := fun i => (σ i).val` specialization of the already-public arbitrary-injective theorem `ConditionallyIIDWith.map`, and `exchangeableAt` can use the more general block/prefix theorem instead.

summary: One new public helper is a permuted-prefix specialization of the existing arbitrary-injective finite-block API, so it exposes duplicate API at the wrong level of generality.
